question.text: As per SEBI's circular of October 2024 (effective November 20, 2024), what restriction was placed on weekly index options?

answer.text: Each exchange can offer weekly expiry options on only ONE benchmark index
Weekly options were completely banned across all exchanges
Weekly options were allowed on all indices without any restriction
Weekly options were restricted to only institutional investors

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI's October 2024 circular (effective November 20, 2024) mandated that each exchange can have weekly expiry options on ONLY ONE benchmark index. This means: - NSE: can choose only one index for weekly options (e.g., Nifty 50) - BSE: can choose only one index for weekly options (e.g., Sensex)
Purpose: To curb excessive speculative trading in weekly options.
**The Trap:** Weekly options were NOT banned. They were restricted to one benchmark index per exchange.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is a recent regulatory change highly likely to appear in exams.
**Key Takeaway:** SEBI (Oct 2024, effective Nov 20, 2024): ONE weekly index option per exchange only.
